title: Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple
tagline: Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Mahathobhara Yelluru Shree Vishweshwara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Vishweshwara (Shiva) in the Yellur village of Udupi district in the state of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Mahathobhara Yelluru Shree Vishweshwara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Vishweshwara (Shiva) in the Yellur village of Udupi district in the state of Karnataka, India. Vishweshwara is referred to at least in 12 rock edicts and recorded by the Kaifiyaths.

topPlaces.name: Yellur, Udupi
topPlaces.description: Yellur is a village in Udupi District in the state of Karnataka in India. Yellur and its surrounding places were acquired to set up a coal-based thermal power station, the Udupi Power Plant.

topPlaces.name: Kemundel
topPlaces.description: Kemundel (Kemundelu) is a place in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. It is near to Yellur and Admar.

topPlaces.name: Udupi Power Plant
topPlaces.description: Udupi Power Plant is a coal based thermal power station in Karnataka, India, established in 2008. It is located to the north of Mangalore, west of Belmannu, and just to the northeast of Padubidri in the village of Yellur, Udupi district, close to the Shambhavi River, roughly 7 or 8 km (4.3 or 5.0 mi) from the coast from where the imported coal for the power plant is transported to plant through rail line.

topPlaces.name: Adamaru
topPlaces.description: Adamaru or Adamaar or Admar is a village in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. The village houses one of the Ashta Mathas known as Adamaru Matha founded by Shri Madhvacharya, the Dvaita philosopher.

topPlaces.name: Nandicoor
topPlaces.description: Nandicoor (variously Nandikur or Nandikoor) is a village in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. The Nandicoor village lies amidst paddy fields, coconut gardens and evergreen forests.

topPlaces.name: Yermal
topPlaces.description: Yermal is a settlement in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India, between Uchila and Padubidri on National Highway 66. There are two parts of Yermal, one is Tenka (meaning "south" in Tulu) and the other Bada or Badagu (meaning "north" in Tulu).

topPlaces.name: 108 Kalthur
topPlaces.description: 108 Kalthur is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Udupi taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Santhoor Subramanya Temple
topPlaces.description: Santhoor Subramanya Temple is a Hindu temple located in the village of Santhoor near Padubidri, Karnataka. Here Kartikeya is worshipped as Subramanya, lord of all serpents.

items.note: Yellur is a village in Udupi District in the state of Karnataka in India. Yellur and its surrounding places were acquired to set up a coal-based thermal power station, the Udupi Power Plant.
Kemundel (Kemundelu) is a place in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. It is near to Yellur and Admar.

Udupi Power Plant is a coal based thermal power station in Karnataka, India, established in 2008. It is located to the north of Mangalore, west of Belmannu, and just to the northeast of Padubidri in the village of Yellur, Udupi district, close to the Shambhavi River, roughly 7 or 8 km (4.3 or 5.0 mi) from the coast from where the imported coal for the power plant is transported to plant through rail line.

Adamaru or Adamaar or Admar is a village in the Udupi district of the state of Karnataka, India. The village houses one of the Ashta Mathas known as Adamaru Matha founded by Shri Madhvacharya, the Dvaita philosopher.
Nandicoor (variously Nandikur or Nandikoor) is a village in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India. The Nandicoor village lies amidst paddy fields, coconut gardens and evergreen forests.

Yermal is a settlement in Udupi district of Karnataka state in India, between Uchila and Padubidri on National Highway 66. There are two parts of Yermal, one is Tenka (meaning "south" in Tulu) and the other Bada or Badagu (meaning "north" in Tulu).
108 Kalthur is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Udupi taluk of Udupi district in Karnataka.
Santhoor Subramanya Temple is a Hindu temple located in the village of Santhoor near Padubidri, Karnataka. Here Kartikeya is worshipped as Subramanya, lord of all serpents.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (26 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (35 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Yelluru Shri Vishweshwara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Yellur, Udupi, Kemundel, Udupi Power Plant, Adamaru, Nandicoor.
